topic: OMNIX Code Topology Graph, Guided Onboarding Tours, and Ripple-Effect Impact Guard
context: We researched the seventh reference library, Understand-Anything, to integrate codebase visualization, structured guided tours, and recursive impact analysis into OMNIX Workbench.
options_considered: Rely solely on standard chat messages and text-based git diffs to verify agent outputs and explore project structures.

Implement: 1. A hybrid Tree-sitter (deterministic parsing) + LLM (semantic summaries) codebase knowledge graph builder; 2. Automated dependencies-ordered Guided Onboarding Tours; 3. Ripple-Effect Impact Guard that intersects git diff paths, crawls the dependency graph, references history lessons in `ai-memory.json`, and triggers alerts for high-risk changes before approval.
chosen_option: Implement Tree-sitter hybrid graph builder, Guided Onboarding, and the Ripple-Effect Impact Guard.
reasoning: Visualizing ripple effects transforms code review: Developers often approve agent refactoring files (like helper utilities) without realizing it breaks 5 downstream components. Marking impacted paths in a visual graph before clicking Confirm makes the code review process highly defensive and safe.

Pre-emptive Error Prevention: By intersecting the recursive impact analysis with the Anti-Failure Memory Bank (`ai-memory.json`), the system can automatically block changes if a file edit triggers a path that historically caused production crashes or bugs.

Fast Local Parsing: Running Tree-sitter natively in Rust (Tauri core) avoids generating heavy API bills while scanning files, ensuring rapid incremental topological rebuilds during local developer sessions.
tradeoffs: Generating semantic summaries for huge legacy codebases (1000+ files) can still consume excessive API tokens: resolved by running Tree-sitter for structural link generation first, and lazily fetching LLM semantic summaries only when the developer selects a specific node.
impact_scope: Tauri code scanner (Rust Tree-sitter AST parser, dependency compiler)
Frontend visualizer (force-directed D3.js workspace graph, guided onboarding view)
future_risks: Parsing dynamic features in interpreted languages (like JavaScript closures or reflection) can lead to incomplete edges: resolved by combining static import/export dependency edges with runtime file change signatures as a heuristic fallback.
